text: The surgeon locates the plane between the prostate and the neurovascular bundles, carefully preserving the nerves to maintain their function.

The surgeon carefully peels the neurovascular bundles away from the surrounding tissue layer, avoiding thermal injury by using clips near the nerves. Blood vessels are cauterized as they are encountered, ensuring a clear surgical field.
The surgeon clips the blood vessels near the prostate, ensuring the nerve remains undisturbed and positioned away from the surgical field. Additional clips are applied to secure the vessels.
